content.text:
I couldn’t find any property risks attached to the Microsoft 2026 pricing node.

What I checked
- Opened Cornerstone/Microsoft/2026 (Microsoft 2026 pricing).
- The pricing content exists (metadata: global property reinsurance, inception 2026-01-01 → 2026-12-31, LOB=PROP, broker=Orion, primary insurer=Sentinel, status=Bound).
- There are no child nodes under Cornerstone/Microsoft/2026 and the Pricing node has no mapped PropertyRisk data collection (attempt to read Cornerstone/Microsoft/2026/data/PropertyRisk failed — collections not mapped).

Next steps — pick one
1. I can run a broader search for any PropertyRisk or risk items that mention Microsoft across the mesh.  
2. I can ask MeshAgent to inspect whether property risks are stored elsewhere or why the collection is not mapped. (Delegate)  
3. If you want, I can hand this to the Executor to create/import property-risk entries under the pricing node. (Handoff — requires your confirmation)

Which would you like me to do?
